author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
—
Now, the Anna Tolle case is an interesting one. There are some Crew Cards that are actually “Crew Card Packs”, containing a single Crew Card. 

This is due to those cards being awarded to players way back, when the team had not yet figured out what their Crew rollout would look like.

So, Anna Tolle is one of the 5 examples of this. You still need to “unpack” (or roll) that SFT Crew Card Pack, to reveal the unique Crew Card (cNFT) within.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: When you do, you now can no longer trade this card on the Galactic Marketplace, but now have to resort to Tensor

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
While at it, and to be fully complete: Semi-Fungible Tokens are basically the exact same as Fungible Tokens (Solana, USDC, Atlas, etc), but they adhere to a special convention.

This convention is that they can not have any decimals. In other words there can not be any fractions. This makes sense as we can not trade 0.3 Fimbul Airbikes. The minimum number of an SFT someone can own is 1. This is clearly unlike regular FTs, where you can own 0.0001 if you wanted to.

author: Odicerous
content: It is technical. Here is the full story:  The Star Atlas marketplace supports only SFTs (Semi-Fungible Tokens), and not true NFTs (Non-Fungible Tokens) or cNFTS (the compressed version of NFTs).  Unlike NFTs, SFTs are not unique. Your Crew pack/ship/badge/iron ore is exactly the same as mine.   Because of this property, SFTs can be stacked (most wallets will show them as such) in the sense that you own 5 Fimbul Airbikes. There is no need to separate them out, which definitely would not work for Crew Cards (cNFTs).  In short: no support for (c)NFTs means unique assets can be or be listed/sold on the SA marketplace(s). Therefore the team has partnered with Tensor to take care of this regarding their Crew Cards. Tensor supports NFTs, but not SFTs, so they complement each other.  In the future, the team will fix this, and they will add NFT support to their own marketplace. But this has been put on hold as with Tensor’s help this was no longer an obstacle.

author: 𝐋ᴀɴᴢᴇʀ [SPS]
content: Hmm.. well.. NFTs are the true NFTs  They are unique and can therefore not be stacked. Any one of your Crew Cards will have different stats from any other Crew Card you “rolled”.  SFTs are really an in-between. Neither fully FT, but certainly not an NFT either. Just a cheap way to create something that is not unique, but can not be fractionalized. Btw, the same asset class (SFT) is used for resources in the game. You can never have half an Iron Ore, for example    So, I would not want to call SFTs “true” NFTs  But sure, you can say that cryptocurrency tokens are FTs indeed

author: ZiggyMUD
content: This refinancing used to happen every 4 years which explained the market cycles but now most governments have borrowed on the short end and need to keep refinancing this debt year after year for the foreseeable future until they decide to refinance on the long end, but the problem is the long end is more expensive and they have no control of it the bond market does not the central banks. Why do you think governments are lowering rates right now? It's less to do with us and more to do with them and not having their budgets blow out when they refinance their debt at higher rates IMO.

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
Honestly, I don’t want to sound pessimistic, but I don’t think Fleet Command will bring in the masses. At the end of the day, it’s basically a reskin (a beautiful one, indeed) of a strategy game that has quite high requirements in both investment and knowledge. When you first approach the game, you don’t know or care about the rent system — you just want something that feels like it’s yours. 
The real news with FC would be bringing Holosim/SAGE (if policies allow it) on mobile, that would open a HUGE playerbase. Imagine having 50-100k players for Holosim paying for the battlepass?

If I had to bet, I’d say that some advanced game mode in UE will be the real deal. The trending game right now is ARC Raiders, and I was already theorizing about the Extraction Shooter genre being very promising for NFT games a year ago. (⁠archived-foundation-room⁠)

Even now, we’re seeing way more new players coming for the UE game than for SAGE itself, and I’d say that’s totally normal and understandable.
UE game modes are the real way to scale the player base!

My 2 cents and I would really like to be proved wrong btw ahahah XD

content: I think I agree on FC. Extraction games for me are a turn off. I tried some never stick. Here still for the MMORPG. author: 𝐋ᴀɴᴢᴇʀ [SPS]
content: Pivots yes but not in the way that this article describes. Star Atlas pivots were through product iterations and testing the blockchain tech, networking, UE5, Web GL assets and their limitations. Also the team had to pivot based on runway after FTX as a means of survival. Definitely a lengthy story. As an example here is SCREAM which has evolved into what we will soon call Fleet Command (SCREAM was before SAGE/SAGE : Starbased and before the team had built any of the needed blockchain tech).

author: [SH]b0b [STAR]
content:
Guys, let's be honest, we have two options: 
-the market sentiment changes and we're all fabulously rich again;
-ATMTA is trying to solve its own problems with an airdrop campaign.

Keeping that in mind: 
Higher  atlas/polis staking multiplier = coin price increase (questionable if we're talking about Polis, since it's PVP, but should works great on Atlas)
Higher Sage multiplier = support for the existing player base (good option. obviously for all of us who is still playing, but difficult to make it fair. Every whale will think they are getting much less then they invested (compare exp to wwap and you will not be happy), everyone who is small will think whales are eating the biggest piece of pie)
Higher purchase multiplier = additional funds for development (again, very questionable, the current player base seems depleted)
Higher multiplier for free modes = player base expansion. But the quality of those who come here to farm airdrops isn't entirely clear. It's possible, and most likely, they're just extractors. But we are not recruiting players that will invest 100k+ dollars after doing a couple of races in UE5, we need to make HYPE aroung z.ink release so its not worst option, tbh.

I don't have a direct answer on what's best to do, the only thing I'd like to avoid is pure extraction (100 profiles in Holosim between seasons that scan 24/7)

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
Personally I do not really care for Zink airdrop, I take it as a “plus”. I just want the tech to be able to move from current SAGE to a way more engaging product, more similar to a game. 
Getting free money is good but a few hundreds of dollars won’t really change much. Michael said we will get a year/year and a half of free transactions with the airdrop, this means that you can multiply what you consume now in SOL daily by 365-550 and that’s what you will get. If you add that Zink is intended to be 100 cheaper than SOL to reduce the txs fees, we are talking about a pretty irrelevant amount. 
No reason to stress ourselves out!

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content:
Mixed arguments about different angles on zink...

My main concern is to have an airdrop campaign that doesn't attract poachers. Hence why I brought up the UE5 and Holosim XP gain (they should be really really low).

Now if the point of the campaign is to attract new builders, tying it to SA isn't the play. And if it is to attract new players, I don't see zink being effective for that. Because attracting new players is tied to having a great product in a more finished state. Any sort of airdrop will benefit only 3 audiences: existing community members, new people willing to bet/invest on C4, and pure airdrop scalpers. Casual players won't join because of zink.

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content:
I think some people are missing the big Z.INK picture.

The WORST CASE SCENARIO of Z.ink is a MASSIVE WIN.

The worst case scenario is:  TX fees go down exponentially which will make new smaller players PROFITABLE.

How many players did we lose because of TX fees?  A LOT.

How many players have stopped playing and are waiting in the shadows to come back?  A LOT.

Z.ink creates scalability for small fleet owners.

This alone will be transformational for acquisition and retention of SAGE players.

It solves one of SA's biggest pain points.

The BEST case scenario is:  Z.INK becomes a popular L1 and airdrop farmers do very well.

I see the airdrop as frosting not the cake.

If Z.INK price does go up, it will give more utility for the future $ATLAS locker perks, which will help lock up more $ATLAS... which over time will help the price.

Z.INK creates strong incentives for NEW players to come and test drive various SA products.

It creates incentives for new/old players to buy crew packs and cultivation facilities.

The subscription + Z.INK creates 2 new revenue sources for ATMTA.  Every revenue source for ATMTA is a massive win for everyone.

Massive kudos to the team for not just creating a tech solution to one of our biggest pain points...

But using it as a massive marketing tool, enrolling KOLs and new rev source.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: For my part, even though I have an interest in saying this, I think ATMTA’s classification makes sense because ZXP gains should take into account not only the capital investment effort but also the effort of locking that invested capital. The fact that this investment is either liquid and can be sold instantly (ships), or locked for a period of time (ATLAS/POLIS), matters. So the scale looks right to me: HOLOSIM/UE5 = small ZXP gains, SAGE = medium, and ATLAS/POLIS = high rewards. In this logic, UE5 should earn a bit more than Holosim but less than Sage.

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content: Yeah, I was just thinking about that on my drive to work. What you and any other ATLAS holder should actually do though is unlock your ATLAS once you get to level 20 as you will not receive any more zXP that will carry forward and hold it and spend it on crew once the next stage of the zXP.  I think the play to earn maximum zXP per $ and time spent is to buy crew, play SAGE, sell ATLAS earned and buy more crew or ships.  There will be way less capital allocated to the Galactic Marketplace and therefore every $ or ATLAS spent will earn you more zXP.  The team is likely rewarding zXP weighted towards revenue or to increase player numbers Holosim & UE5  I don't think time locked is very valuable aka POLIS Locking commitment.  The team was going to run out of ATLAS to sell in January if asset sales didn't increase so I guess it makes sense.  Positives though, POLIS holders you will get in game utility probably in 12+ months, and you have contributed 50-30% of the team runway for the last 2.5 years.  The DAO will receive a 5% stake in Z.ink and some Z.ink validator rewards (split with whoever does the validating for the DAO) so this is the largest positive and the possible arbitrage.

content: My thoughts: PFPs are very important to players or users of a blockchain or social media platform as they lets them express their own personality.  I think hit PFPs are such a difficult thing to get right that I do not think ATMTA should take this on. Please take learnings from successful platforms like Discord, X, Facebook users get to choose their own PFPs and level ups give you different features that you can apply to your own PFPs. (Users always get the choice to apply these features) This should both be a lower lift for team resources and doesn't try to reinvent the wheel.  You could offer users the option to pick these as default PFPs if they don't already have a PFP that they identify with.   I would like my Z.ink PFP to have something that makes it identify that I'm part of the Z.ink platform (like putting .ink on my user name) but it should not entirely take away from my original PFP character.  To get more eyeballs on Z.ink I would think that you would want users to be willing to change their X or discord PFP to have a Z.ink flare across multiple platforms.

You could offer users the option to pick these as default PFPs if they don't already have a PFP that they identify with. To get more eyeballs on Z.ink I would think that you would want users to be willing to change their X or discord PFP to have a Z.ink flare across multiple platforms.

author: JelankaONI
content:
is there even a point in locking POLIS before atlas starts growing?
If atlas isn’t moving and there’s no real economic activity, then polis has nothing to capture value from.
The whole cycle starts with atlas because it reacts faster and has liquidity.
Once the economy actually wakes up and atlas has demand, then locking polis makes sense, since governance and taxes start to matter only at that stage.
why lock the governance token before the economy it governs is active?

author: ZiggyMUD
content:
Once Star Atlas becomes successful if that is your thoughts, then the team will issue more ships to meet demand, to earn more revenue. There are lots of ships still to release.
As the team collects more revenue from players in USDC & ATLAS, the team will sell that ATLAS to pay for development. The selling of ATLAS if Star Atlas is successful will continue to happen forever IMO
Once ATLAS hits the end of its inflation curve I would be surprised that it doesn't get extended in some way.
Land, skins, crew, all assets will be created as demand increases.

I understand this is a long shot, but so are all large asset purchases at this point in the market. If you are still here you you understand what the team is building.
If everyone thought of POLIS as a scarce asset, 51% of the supply is already locked or held by the team, all new issuance of POLIS is to POLIS Stakers and if Star Atlas becomes successful, what are large investors or players want to invest in, where will all the revenue flow through, what will players who want to have political control going to invest in?

I believe POLIS will be less valuable until its not, the goal is to grab the largest part of the pie through staking rewards, continuous Z.ink airdrops, etc, before that moment.

author: ZiggyMUD
content:
The airdrops for the next two years after TGE. Once the Z.ink token has a value it will be easier to estimate how big the airdrop pie will be, could incentivize more players to play Star Atlas buying ATLAS and assets to play, that would lead to more Z.ink network activity, which should help increase the value of the Z.ink token, making the airdrop worth more, driving more players to play, etc.
If the ATLAS price starts trending up with great, repeatable gameplay, with little to no transactions costs so that all players can play, with combate and territory control, we could see prices move like ORE is.
At that point Star Atlas could reach escape velocity

Are new players aware that a new public wallet address is being created for them?

Just feedback as we are in the testing phase

author: ROME | Aegir
content: So what happened was that since FIC sales suck so much and they literally get filled within seconds I had to rush as soon as I saw that they were refilled.  I checked on EveEye and saw there were 700K Starpath Node still available for sale at MRZ-20.  Went to SAGE and and verified they were available.  Checked inventory, back to the market and literally as I was clicking sell, the last got filled and I clicked on the Scam account sell since it moved up on the list.  Knew with a fraction of a second what happened, tried to do a quick refresh of the browser hoping it didn't go through and unfortunately it did.  All happened within seconds or fraction of seconds.  If the FIC process wasn't so broken and we didn't have seconds every few weeks to sell these things then the problem would have been avoided.

author: [AEP] Mendizo
content:
Let's solve the root of the problem (UI, UX, FIC redemption construct, etc. issues). We can't keep playing whackamole with the symptoms, because that never ends. The onus needs to be on the team to address this, especially when it's been highlighted so clearly. There are relatively easy solves too as many suggested (different font for the official wallet, now allowing non-team orders). 

I don't agree at all with the spirit of what the "scammers" are doing here, but it's within the construct that the team allows right now. It's a slippery slope if we end up trying to freeze assets and whatnot when people are operating within what's technically allowed. author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
Circulating could be as high as 60%: 20% ATMTA, 5% SA DAO, 10% O.rigin Airdrop, 15% on a validator deal (though 10% would be recovered after 2 years), and 10% from a TGE if we end up pursuing a public token sale. That 10% would come from the "z.ink team" allocation.

However, actual circulating is closer to that 10% O.rigin airdrop amount + 10% token sale if it happens. The ATMTA, DAO, and validator balances are all delegated and staked.

reply.preview: Message could not be loaded

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: zXP progression continues after this first season airdrop campaign. We tentatively have plans for three additional seasons beyond this one, with a cumulative time frame of 2 years total. In periods without an airdrop, you will still earn progression to your zProfile, but that progression would not apply to future airdrops. Just as we are not retroactively applying any previous SA actions to this current airdrop season. However, the Multipass track should be running regularly without downtime. And there's also strong potential, imo, to attract other projects that have a desire to utilize z.ink as a distribution platform. So there could be intertemporal airdrop periods with rewards going out that don't consist of a ZINK token allocation.

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content:
ORE is a recent example of a fair launch (mining is the only source of tokens) and Ziggy will tell you the pricing is "chef's kiss" my only concern is the actual utility of that token. Since it's you simply betting on what number will "pop up" next. I've also seen chains drop a ton of tokens and price simply plummets because there's more sell pressure than buy.

Right now if we compare pure transactions you're saying 20 TPS is what you expect.

20 TPS × 60 seconds × 60 minutes × 24 hours = 1,728,000 transactions per day

Using just the base fee (0.000005 Z.INK):

Daily Z.INK usage: 1,728,000 × 0.000005 = 8.64 Z.INK per day
Monthly Z.INK usage: 8.64 × 30 = 259.2 Z.INK per month...

Token Supply:

Max supply: 1,000,000,000 Z.INK
Airdrop allocation: 20% = 200,000,000 Z.INK

Chain Operating Costs at 20 TPS (base fees only):

Daily consumption: 8.64 Z.INK
Monthly consumption: 259.2 Z.INK
Yearly consumption: 3,153.6 Z.INK

How long the airdrop can sustain the chain:
200,000,000 Z.INK ÷ 8.64 Z.INK per day = 23,148,148 days

That's approximately 63,419 years (dang maybe its on purpose?)

200,000,000 Z.INK ÷ 259.2 Z.INK per month = 771,604 months

Now if we assume we can sustain 600 TPS it changes to 2,114 years on a 20% airdrop alone. Sure some of that airdrop will be staked and some lost in wallets. But dumping too much in the beginning is also not great is all. I don't have a phd nor can I provide concrete proof of what I'm basing this on so sure take it as you will.

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content: Now let's say we reduced the total supply by a HUGE 1,000 times... so only 1 milly. That still means 2 years at 600 TPS from the airdrop alone.

author: SCORMUD
content: It didn’t really make sense to use one of the two existing tokens to launch a new blockchain. We can assume there will be greater adoption and more transaction overall, especially since the base fees are lower compared to Solana. This should ultimately benefit both ATLAS and POLIS: ATLAS, because trading volumes are likely to increase with future adoption driven by lower base fees; POLIS, because part of the ZINK value distributed to the DAO will be redirected through governance. And who knows, maybe one day, once U.S. regulations become clearer, a percentage of ZINK could even be redistributed to POLIS lockers. And why not ATLAS, to a lesser extent.

author: SCORMUD
content:
At the launch of the UNI token in September 2020, Uniswap distributed a total of 400 million UNI out of the 1 billion UNI tokens created.
Here’s the full breakdown 

 Total Supply: 1,000,000,000 UNI

60% (600 million UNI) — Allocated to the Uniswap community
400 million UNI were retroactively airdropped to early users.
200 million UNI reserved for future community programs (liquidity mining, grants, etc.)
21.51% (215 million UNI) — For the team (4-year vesting period)
17.8% (178 million UNI) — For investors
0.69% (6.9 million UNI) — For advisors

 Initial Community Distribution (400 million UNI)

150 million UNI distributed to all users who interacted with Uniswap before September 1, 2020
→ Each eligible address received 400 UNI (worth roughly $1,200 USD at launch).
49 million UNI distributed to historical liquidity providers
100 million UNI reserved for future liquidity mining incentives
The remaining UNI allocated to other community initiatives

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: If you are looking purely live transactions per second, Z.ink currently sits roughly #16 out of all block chains.  Sitting below Arbitrum and above Ethereum.    Polygon, Sui, and Arbitrum all have slightly more live TPS then Z.ink/Star Atlas, and sitting at marketcap of 7B, 9.4B, and 10B respectively.  (not price adjusted for price changes but point should still be clear)    Z.ink also comes with transactions baked into the chain, e.g. Star Atlas, so it doesn't have a cold start program of having no one on the chain.  It will immediately be top 20 by TPS of all blockchains in the world.  Hopium would put Z.ink around 7-10B.    But realistically... without having a z.ink ecosystem (swaps, defi, etc) then maybe initial marketcap could be 100-500M?  It will all depend on what agreement ATMTA/Zink can put in place for other teams to build on the z.ink chain.  By the numbers... it looks pretty damn good.

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content: A 100M marketcap being at the lower end of the valuation seems still pretty high to me   We know that 10% of that will be airdropped op day 1 (unlocked, unstaked), which would mean $10 million worth of tokens (in that case - probably one of the biggest airdrops ever? Or the biggest?).  Looking at the numbers shared by Chris (see attached screenshot), that would mean —using current A.LPHA participant total— the top 10% will (on average) get 42k tokens, which means the airdrop would earn them $42k . The next 10% tranche would get $22k (on average).  Now, clearly the goal is to blow the participant number up. If we can 10x that number, a 100M marketcap may start to become more realistic. If it stays below, then I anticipate the selling pressure will be so big that the marketcap will crash down on day 1.  Note that in the event of a sell-off, a lack of liquidity will compound the downward price pressure on top.  All that to say that my guess is that the higher the (USD value of the) individual rewards, the more people will sell (a good chunk), and the lower the marketcap after day 1.  Unless we really draw in huge numbers into the campaign, a 100M marketcap seems unattainable on day 1.  Would love to hear your thoughts on this!

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content: Ever wonder how people come up with these numbers? Like how does one establish a value for these things?... I tend to usually ask myself would I pay X amount for said token and if you multiple this by the circulating supply what does that equal. Then consider the liquidity, the wash trading, the market making. Does the volume seem artificial or genuine?. It's sooo easy on chains with low fees to fake volume. Just spin up new wallets to send tokens too your only loss is the very very small fee. Factor in who get's those fees and who benefits from the making of the market and how much tokens they got for free and you get yourself a wildfire (or at the very least a facade of something nice)

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
I think tournaments are streamable, but the average gameplay isn’t very engaging yet. Some games are objectively hard to stream (like strategy or RPGs). You really need a strong community for that. Either you add some roleplay elements to the game or you wait a bit longer until it becomes more stream-friendly.

The main issue right now is the repetition of the same actions. You either need a lot more content in the game or a ranked system. Neither of those is currently feasible.

The new patch is incredible—absolutely jaw-dropping. Let the team cook, we’re right on track, we just need a little bit more of time!

If someone earns $1,000 from the ZINK airdrop, they might use that money to start playing SAGE. author: SCORMUD
content:
I think new players will come naturally once the ZINK blockchain becomes operational.
It doesn’t necessarily need to be profitable for an XXS player — it just needs to cost them nothing, or very close to nothing.
Airdrops are farmed, and most players simply sell their airdropped tokens and move on to farm the next one somewhere else.
What really matters is the marketing effect, the announcement that “everyone can join for free to farm the ZINK airdrop.”
Whether people farm small or large amounts doesn’t really change the outcome — only a small portion will actually stay anyway.
The real adoption should happen once ZINK is fully operational.
The “airdrop for everyone” message looks good, it’s great for visibility and marketing, but that’s not where true adoption will take place.

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content:
At 600 TPS, here's what validators would be earning:
Transaction Fee Distribution:

Total daily fees: 259.2 Z.INK
50% burned: 129.6 Z.INK
50% to validators: 129.6 Z.INK per day

Validator Earnings Breakdown:

Daily: 129.6 Z.INK
Monthly: 3,888 Z.INK
Yearly: 47,304 Z.INK

Important considerations:

This 129.6 Z.INK daily would be distributed among ALL validators based on their stake weight
If you had 100 validators with equal stake, each would earn ~1.296 Z.INK per day
In reality, validators with more stake would earn proportionally more.. 

it ain't a whole lot for fees.. But depending on how much Z.INK is actually in circulation and not credits (which occurs when credits -> fees -> validators) it maybe enough. Especially post 2 month window.

author: [SLY] anthonyraEcon Vantarah
content: Most private blockchains work on a proof of authority because there's no need to be "decentralized" with two parties who use it. It's also a lot harder to give up power once you have it.. but they're the ones doing the things. I didn't mean it as a stab, just that since they do control and plan to already they'll be the sole fee collectors.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: heyo.  A $10M airdrop is tiny.  Its not the biggest airdrop ever and I'm not sure it would make top 100.  But lets run with that number.  $10m circulating is ridiculously small.   The less tokens in circulation, the more the price goes up.   And we know people are going to be staking tokens.  So... whats a fair assessment?  Do we REALLY think everyone is going to instantly sell? I don't think so.  So lets take 50% selling and 50% staking.  That leaves 5M tokens in circulation.  Let's say 2.5m is sold the first day, suddenly thats 75% of coins out of circulation,  the price would absolutely explode, would people pay for it?   And then, It won't take much for a whale to literally come in and buy the entire remaining pot.  And then we have 0% in circluation?  Would that mean z.ink is worth infinite?  For a L1 chain 100M marketcap just doesn't make much sense when viewed from an airdrop perspective... I think we hit 500M-1B which leaves 50M circulating from the airdrop.  Sounds much more reasonable.

For a L1 chain 100M marketcap just doesn't make much sense when viewed from an airdrop perspective... I think we hit 500M-1B which leaves 50M circulating from the airdrop. Sounds much more reasonable.

author: [COEX] BulutONI
content:
One of the biggest problems for many regular players who can’t get the Sage interface to work is that they’re unable to unload the items inside their ships. Because of this, everything stored in the ships ends up being lost.
Ships are listed at certain time intervals, but since the interface doesn’t function properly, regular players can’t rehire them. Meanwhile, players who use bots can easily rent any ship they want.

This situation is especially difficult for small players. For example, if a ship has 5 items left inside, and each requires 0.002 SOL to manage, that’s 0.01 SOL in total — about 3,270 ATLAS.
If a player loses access to 10 rental fleets this way, that’s a loss of around 32,700 ATLAS.

I hope a quick solution can be found for this issue.

author: 《VΛ》 KATIREcon Vantarah
content: To clarify, Maharog isn't the name of the game mode, it's still called Galia. Maharog will be more of an in-game event.  The answer to the second question is a solid nope. The blockchain team is very clear that single player game modes can never give any on-chain rewards, because there's no way to prevent cheating when it isn't running on a server. So Galia is purely for fun, no blockchain integration at all.  To answer your first question, we hope that to be the case in a limited fashion. The main technical issue preventing us from placing enemies on the surfaces of planets, for example, is that Unreal's AI navigation system is fundamentally incompatible with round planets, only flat worlds (which 99.9% of games are). So we will have to build a completely bespoke system for ai navigation on our planets and it won't be an easy task. It took Star Citizen many years to do, and they have 50x our resources. But we are working on it. We can, however, spawn enemy ships anywhere we like. So ships can be anywhere, but NPCs can only be on space stations or underground on planets (where we can fake it, again like Star Citizen did initially), not on planet surfaces

author: [Coex] BidobuONI
content: I'm not sure what you are asking here, but: 1) Never ever import the seed you used for your Ledger into a hot wallet. When you do that you basically throw away the extra security your Ledger provided you 2) You can not move your POLIS stake to your Ledger. There is no workaround, this is simply not possible. 3) Yes, you can connect your Ledger wallet to dApps through a wallet extension, such as Phantom. However, this does not change the previous answer, and is unrelated that question

author: ZiggyMUD
content:
@SW4GN3R Some ideas:
Early Check-In Fee (Dynamic & % of zXP)
Let users pay to check in early for Z.ink streaks — the farther from your window's start, the steeper the fee. Tie the fee to a % of total zXP so high-streak holders pay meaningfully to protect their progress.
Use Case: Going off-grid? Hiking, skiing, no signal, or away from your Ledger? Many might gladly pay ATLAS/USDC or sacrifice zXP to save a multi-month streak.

Bonus: Built-in Streak Insurance via Tiers
Each Z.ink plan includes X "streak-save" days per month.
Example: My streak reset after 10 days despite daily logins (about the same time, same site). Others might have reported this too. I’d have paid heavily to keep my streak alive — whether glitch or user error.  A subscription with streak protection = stress-free retention + new revenue. Users buy peace of mind.

author: [SH]b0b [STAR]
content: I think the Titan might have something to do with it.  It's a very expensive asset, generates a lot, but through only 1 transaction.  I suspect some people are gaming the system for XP, by using small fleets to go through more transactions.  Which also brings in much higher SOL costs.  In my mind, XP should be tied to output of whatever transactions are done, not the transaction count itself. But that might be a much harder calculation for the team. And also, people would just whine and say they're rewarding whales.

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content: I'd say it's not just a titan owner issue. Taking Bryan as an example, he's a player with both a large fleet and a large number of locked tokens. It doesn't matter whether you use WVAP or current market value. His fleet is worth ~$230,000(number from eveeye portfolio page), while his tokens are worth $31,000 for Atlas and $51,000 for Polis. Tokens are worth three times less than assets, but they provide roughly the same amount of experience. And that's not even taking into account that tokens is 100% passive exp income and sage assets need daily management and have huge sol fees cost.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: Yep a flat to rising ATLAS price is a win for everyone. Depending on the Z.ink TGE ATLAS and POLIS will get repriced based on the 2 years of airdrops.  Both SVM L2s and privacy token FDVs are majority north of $200m into the billions. With 10% of the Z.ink token supply being airdropped to the die-hard Star Atlas community how would these tokens be repriced if the airdrop is worth $10m, $30m or even $100m, and how much of those $ could be used to massively extend Star Atlas development or incentivize players to buy ATLAS or POLIS or buy assets and play the game?  I'm not trying to create crazy hopium but I ask everyone to look at the comparable blockchains and then tell me what kind of usage they have. What if the Swiss trip inked some kind of great partnership or deal for Z.ink?  Lots of unknowns but the potential for both ATMTA and Star Atlas could be huge with great execution and gaming products.

I'm not trying to create crazy hopium but I ask everyone to look at the comparable blockchains and then tell me what kind of usage they have. What if the Swiss trip inked some kind of great partnership or deal for Z.ink? Lots of unknowns but the potential for both ATMTA and Star Atlas could be huge with great execution and gaming products.

content:
@SW4GN3RI really like the approach used in ATOM.
In our Discord, when you receive the welcome message, it sends you directly to the tutorial, to the classic questions a new user might ask, to the useful links, and to the “start here” section.
It’s something we could also adopt here on Star Atlas or on ZINK
(while being extremely careful with scammers!!!!!!)

What type of player are you?
A player, a player-investor, or just an investor?
And from there you can start with:
UE5 – crew – XXS – INK – Atlas

Are you a player-investor?
Holosim – StarBase – Polis

Only an investor?
Rent – Marketplace – Claim Stakes

Or something along those lines, anyway.
I’ve also been reading Aephia’s articles for years now. Very useful.

Creating some guidelines for new players could really help.

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: Turbine bottlenecks around 100M CU. XDP will help solve that.  The real problem is we aren't anywhere near to 100M CU right now. Our blocks are 20-50% full. Occasionally hitting  100% but on very rare occasions.  And in order to get more customers we need faster finalization, cancel/rebid, multiple concurrent leaders, and a lot more. Then we can have Nasdaq and proper order books.

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: New grok version of web 3 gaming narrative is a good perspective

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content:
Honest Thoughts on Solana Web3 Gaming (Star Atlas & the Broader Ecosystem)
Yes, the Web3/crypto gaming space has been brutally negative for the last 2–3 years — and rightfully so in many cases. 2021–2022 was pure speculation: Axie Infinity mooned to a $10B valuation on Ponzi-like yield farming, then collapsed 99%. Most “play-to-earn” games were glorified token farms with zero fun gameplay. When the bear market hit, 95% of those projects died or became irrelevant. Daily active users across all blockchain games plummeted from ~2M in early 2022 to under 500k for long stretches. Sentiment went from “this will replace Steam” to “Web3 gaming is dead forever.”
But here’s the key shift in 2025: The narrative is moving from “play-to-earn” (farm tokens → dump) to “play-and-own” (actual fun games where ownership is a bonus, not the only reason to play). Solana is at the absolute center of that pivot for three big reasons:
    1    Tech that finally works for real games
    ◦    Sub-second finality, < $0.001 fees, 50k+ TPS (and climbing with Firedancer/Alpenglow upgrades).
    ◦    No more “wait 30 seconds for your sword to mint” or “raid crashes because gas spiked.”
    ◦    Compressed NFTs + state compression = massive worlds with millions of assets for pennies.
    2    Actual products shipping (not just whitepapers)
    ◦    Star Atlas specifically: After the FTX collapse nearly killed them, the team downsized, survived on asset sales, and is still targeting a core AAA release (Season 0 / SAGE full version on UE5) by end of 2025. They just ran the Golden Carnival event (Nov 2024 – Feb 2025) with $4M in prizes, open-sourced tools for Solana devs, and are integrating AI agents. The browser-based Golden Era/SAGE already has on-chain economy elements running smoothly. It’s delayed forever (classic), but they’re one of the few 2021 survivors still shipping updates weekly.
    3    Ecosystem momentum that’s undeniable right now
    ◦    Solana launched the Play Solana PSG1 handheld console (Oct 2025) — built-in wallet, biometric security, native dApp store. 150k+ pre-orders = real consumer demand.
    ◦    Official @GamingOnSolana_ hub + Game Pass Season 1 (Starboard) uniting 30+ titles with cross-game rewards, leaderboards, and Aura NFTs.
    ◦    Dev activity exploded: 7,600 new developers in 2024 alone. Games like Aurory, Nyan Heroes, Genopets, Mini Royale, Valannia, etc., are live and updating constantly.
    ◦    Market forecasts: Web3 gaming projected to grow from ~$25–30B in 2024/25 to $65–125B by 2030–2032 (multiple reports from FMI, SNS Insider, etc.), with huge CAGRs (19–70% depending on the source).
Will It “Come Back to Life”?
Short answer: Yes — but it will look completely different (and better) than the 2021 hype cycle. We’re already seeing the early stages of the comeback in late 2025:
    •    Sentiment on X/Twitter has flipped from “Web3 gaming is dead” to “I believe in the Solana gaming supercycle” (thousands of posts in the last month alone).
    •    Institutional money quietly flowing back in (Pantera, Galaxy, etc.).
    •    Traditional gamers starting to notice because the games are finally fun first, crypto second.
2026–2027 outlook in my view:
    •    Base case: Solana captures 30–50% of the next Web3 gaming wave. Star Atlas launches properly → becomes the “EVE Online of crypto” if they nail the economy. Overall sector 5–10x from here as mobile/handheld adoption explodes.
    •    Bull case: PSG1/Seeker phones go mainstream → millions of normies accidentally own NFTs through casual games → new all-time highs for gaming tokens.
    •    Risks still exist: Another macro bear market, regulatory hits, or if teams revert to extractive tokenomics.
Bottom line: The negativity was earned, but the tech and teams have matured. Solana fixed the biggest pain point (performance), and projects like Star Atlas prove you can survive crypto winter if you’re actually building.

content:
My Thoughts on Star Atlas’ New Zink Chain (z.ink) – Nov 2025 

Star Atlas is launching Zink, a dedicated SVM L1 for gaming (Solana VM compatible). Mainnet December 2025.

Core Features
• ZK privacy + zProfiles (on-chain rep + auto-approve actions)
• Native token: $ZINK (gas + staking)
• Starts semi-centralized → decentralizes over 18-24 mo
• 10% airdrop live (farm XP: play SAGE, lock ATLAS/POLIS, marketplace trades)

Why it’s smart
Fixes Solana congestion for real fleet battles & UE5 MMO,Instant users on day-1 from existing community,zProfiles could be a massive UX win for all Web3 gaming,Ties upside directly to ATLAS/POLIS holders,
The Risks
Another “dead app chain” if UE5 isn’t polished fast,Starts centralized (trust-me-bro roadmap),High execution risk – Star Atlas has been “soon™” since 2021,
Bottom line
One of the highest-conviction plays in Solana gaming right now. If they nail Dec launch + UE5 in early 2026 → top-20 L1 potential & 10-50x for $ZINK/ATLAS/POLIS.Farming the airdrop is basically free money if you already hold or play.

Make-or-break moment for the project – I’m leaning bullish.You farming yet?  @ROME | Earther

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: Honest Thoughts on Solana Web3 Gaming (Star Atlas & the Broader Ecosystem) Yes, the Web3/crypto gaming space has been brutally negative for the last 2–3 years — and rightfully so in many cases. 2021–2022 was pure speculation: Axie Infinity mooned to a $10B valuation on Ponzi-like yield farming, then collapsed 99%. Most “play-to-earn” games were glorified token farms with zero fun gameplay. When the bear market hit, 95% of those projects died or became irrelevant. Daily active users across all blockchain games plummeted from ~2M in early 2022 to under 500k for long stretches. Sentiment went from “this will replace Steam” to “Web3 gaming is dead forever.” But here’s the key shift in 2025: The narrative is moving from “play-to-earn” (farm tokens → dump) to “play-and-own” (actual fun games where ownership is a bonus, not the only reason to play). Solana is at the absolute center of that pivot for three big reasons:     1    Tech that finally works for real games     ◦    Sub-second finality, < $0.001 fees, 50k+ TPS (and climbing with Firedancer/Alpenglow upgrades).     ◦    No more “wait 30 seconds for your sword to mint” or “raid crashes because gas spiked.”     ◦    Compressed NFTs + state compression = massive worlds with millions of assets for pennies.     2    Actual products shipping (not just whitepapers)     ◦    Star Atlas specifically: After the FTX collapse nearly killed them, the team downsized, survived on asset sales, and is still targeting a core AAA release (Season 0 / SAGE full version on UE5) by end of 2025. They just ran the Golden Carnival event (Nov 2024 – Feb 2025) with $4M in prizes, open-sourced tools for Solana devs, and are integrating AI agents. The browser-based Golden Era/SAGE already has on-chain economy elements running smoothly. It’s delayed forever (classic), but they’re one of the few 2021 survivors still shipping updates weekly.     3    Ecosystem momentum that’s undeniable right now     ◦    Solana launched the Play Solana PSG1 handheld console (Oct 2025) — built-in wallet, biometric security, native dApp store. 150k+ pre-orders = real consumer demand.     ◦    Official @GamingOnSolana_ hub + Game Pass Season 1 (Starboard) uniting 30+ titles with cross-game rewards, leaderboards, and Aura NFTs.     ◦    Dev activity exploded: 7,600 new developers in 2024 alone. Games like Aurory, Nyan Heroes, Genopets, Mini Royale, Valannia, etc., are live and updating constantly.     ◦    Market forecasts: Web3 gaming projected to grow from ~$25–30B in 2024/25 to $65–125B by 2030–2032 (multiple reports from FMI, SNS Insider, etc.), with huge CAGRs (19–70% depending on the source). Will It “Come Back to Life”? Short answer: Yes — but it will look completely different (and better) than the 2021 hype cycle. We’re already seeing the early stages of the comeback in late 2025:     •    Sentiment on X/Twitter has flipped from “Web3 gaming is dead” to “I believe in the Solana gaming supercycle” (thousands of posts in the last month alone).     •    Institutional money quietly flowing back in (Pantera, Galaxy, etc.).     •    Traditional gamers starting to notice because the games are finally fun first, crypto second. 2026–2027 outlook in my view:     •    Base case: Solana captures 30–50% of the next Web3 gaming wave. Star Atlas launches properly → becomes the “EVE Online of crypto” if they nail the economy. Overall sector 5–10x from here as mobile/handheld adoption explodes.     •    Bull case: PSG1/Seeker phones go mainstream → millions of normies accidentally own NFTs through casual games → new all-time highs for gaming tokens.     •    Risks still exist: Another macro bear market, regulatory hits, or if teams revert to extractive tokenomics. Bottom line: The negativity was earned, but the tech and teams have matured. Solana fixed the biggest pain point (performance), and projects like Star Atlas prove you can survive crypto winter if you’re actually building.

author: ROME | Winston [ROME]
content:
That vesting with rewards from early sellers is great for price appreciation.  

Think what you want about HEX token but their locker system was great I think. Should take inspiration from it for POLIS lockers. 
Emergency end stake:  End your lock earlier than you said you would, but pay a penalty. Penalty is redistributed to other lockers. If you want to at least regain your initial capital you need to have served 50% of your locking time. 
If price goes up a lot, you can emergency end stake paying a huge fine but still be up on your initial USD value.

You can follow these transfers of tokens through a dashboard. Adds stickiness to locking your POLIS. Feels good to see tokens transfer from "paper hands" to "diamond hands".

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
Interesting discussion!

One thing to keep in mind is that the team is trying to raise funds through the launch of the z.ink blockchain.

If people are thinking about converting their airdrop into anything other than ATLAS or POLIS, then that means it has to go through a liquidity pool that the team will need to fund with their own capital (one way or another).

This means that if they give away z.ink, and a good chunk of the receivers want to convert this to (say) USDC, then the team would have to seed the liquidity pool with substantially more than that amount of  USDC, or risk the token price collapsing on day 1.

In other words, they would have to spend a lot of money (at least short term), which goes completely against their goal of raising money.

So, I wholeheartedly support any mechanic that will see less sales, and more spread out sales (which the market can potentially absorb), so we can prevent bankrupting ATMTA, and instead see money flow to them so they can boost their runway.

Even if that would inconvenience me personally.

author: ROME | Earther [ROME]
content: The team would likely be acting completely different if they had limited runway, without a guarantee of future runway. That would be too risky of a bet,  this far into a cycle as optimistic as Wagner is, he operates ATMTA using data and probabilities. It is a rule to not let a project fall under 12 months of runway because you have so much less bargaining power.   It is more likely that the team realized they were not going to be able to complete all current projects underway and have a comfortable enough amount of runway to then raise more capital.  So I believe they likely acquired a deal whereby if they meet said product releases this would unlock potential capital. If capital is obtainable by delivering product, the only worry you need to have is having the right personnel and time needed to execute on those product releases. This is much more manageable at a time where the market is crashing. It is also likely why it was stated 6 to 8 months. More manpower sooner completion, less manpower later completion.  Also this could highlight why the positivity from both Wagner and the team once Star Atlas reaches those dates.

It is more likely that the team realized they were not going to be able to complete all current projects underway and have a comfortable enough amount of runway to then raise more capital. So I believe they likely acquired a deal whereby if they meet said product releases this would unlock potential capital. If capital is obtainable by delivering product, the only worry you need to have is having the right personnel and time needed to execute on those product releases. This is much more manageable at a time where the market is crashing. It is also likely why it was stated 6 to 8 months. More manpower sooner completion, less manpower later completion. Also this could highlight why the positivity from both Wagner and the team once Star Atlas reaches those dates.

That would be too risky of a bet,  this far into a cycle as optimistic as Wagner is, he operates ATMTA using data and probabilities. author: [SAI] ric_greenman [STAR]
content: Holosim had a different situation, and the fix applied there would actually make this worse. It removed the ability to sell to/buy from specific wallets. Only the best offer/bid (btw, it was possible to work around it on the backend - it was a UI fix, not onchain).  Now imagine only scam orders are up. With Atlas Prime on, it's one click away of messing up, thinking you're going for real orders.  I don't know how to address this, aside from blocking orders below a certain % of Global Market average. But then, it breaks the whole free market idea.
